summary_one_line: Input must form a well-formed sfnt+cmap structure that selects a Format 0 subtable with at least one nonzero glyphIdArray byte.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parseable OpenType/sfnt font header carrying the literal 'cmap' table tag (bytes 63 6d 61 70) plus the structural table-record offsets needed to route into CmapSubtableFormat0::collect_unicodes, where the 256-entry glyphIdArray must hold >=1 nonzero byte so the true branch of `if (glyphIdArray[i])` fires.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner seed contains the magic sfnt header 00 01 00 00 at offset 0 and the ASCII literal 'cmap' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, followed by a run of 0x15 (non-zero) glyphIdArray bytes (offsets 0x1e-0x27); collect_unicodes therefore reaches the true branch and adds glyphs (hitcount 2143, out->add at line 65 [W]).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser seeds are short, unstructured byte salads (sizes 13-62) starting with e0/fe/4c rather than the sfnt magic; they never present the 'cmap' tag or a Format 0 subtable with a nonzero glyphIdArray, so the loop runs but every glyphIdArray[i] is zero (L: T=0 F=2560), keeping the branch on the false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace logs the operands of the structural cmap-routing comparisons (table-tag equality, version/format checks) and substitutes the required literals (the 'cmap' tag, version words) directly into the input from the CMP buffer; plain value_profile has only the CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance gradient and havoc/token mutators with no I2SRandReplace, so it cannot place the multi-byte structural literals that route execution to Format 0 and produce nonzero glyphIdArray entries.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the body of `if (glyphIdArray[i])` inside CmapSubtableFormat0::collect_unicodes.
Only value_profile_cmplog wins this pair via the I2S technique delta.
The winner resolves the branch in 9/10 trials with high hitcount while the loser never resolves it.
The winner-resolving seed carries the ASCII 'cmap' table tag in its header.
The winner seed contains a run of nonzero 0x15 bytes serving as nonzero glyphIdArray entries.
The loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the sfnt magic header.
Under the loser, the blocker loop runs but every glyphIdArray[i] is zero so the true side never fires.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of the havoc/token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5400 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed fd117cd3e66df32b` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 47 of the 48-level ancestor chain; the tool reports '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 47' confirming an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in the winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile (no I2SRandReplace) reaches the true side of `if (glyphIdArray[i])` as often as value_profile_cmplog despite the input needing the exact multi-byte 'cmap' tag and structural offsets — i.e. the CMP_MAP gradient alone places the literals at equal rate.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the decisive literal is specifically the 'cmap' tag equality vs. an earlier sfnt version/offset comparison is inferred from byte position, not from a CMP-level trace of which comparison I2S solved.
